PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-11-2016 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-11-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stephen Picone (via court call) for (objector) Anna Samuel and specially appearing for Michael Martinovsky for Raisha Rikoshinsky.

Mr. Picone reported that they have settled the matter, that they have filed a settlement agreement as well as requests for dismissal. The Court took the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary off calendar.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-09-2015 Continued To Jan-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-09-2015 Continued To Jan-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ryan Ramseyer (via court call) for Anna Samuel and specially appearing for Mr. Martinovsky.

Mr. Ramseyer reported that they have settled the case and that they have a signed settlement agreement. The Court stated that it will continue the matter so that they can file a Petition to approve the settlement. The Petition for letters of administration and the Petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary were continued to January 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-23-2015 Continued To Nov-09-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stephen Picone (via court call) for Ana Samuels. Michael Martinovsky for Raisa Rikoshinsky.

The Court asked about the status of mediation. Mr. Martinovsky reported that mediation was not the best choice, that the parties are now engaged in informal discussions and would like 10 days to try and settle the case. The Court asked about a trial date. Mr. Picone stated that he is having difficulty communicating with his client, that most of the discovery is done except for his clients deposition, that he may need to make a motion to withdraw, that he does not want a trial date set, that they will make every effort to work out a settlement in the next 10 days and requested a 60 day continuance. The Court stated that it will grant the continuance, that if they are back here without resolution they should be prepared for a trial date, Ms. Samuel should have new counsel in place and Mr. Picone's motion should be on calendar. The petition for letters of administration and the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary were continued to November 9, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-23-2015 Continued To Nov-09-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-27-2015 Continued To Sep-23-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar May-27-2015 Continued To Sep-23-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion By Raisa Rikoshinsky To Quash Subpoena To Merrill Lynch Pierce Fenner & Smith, Inc., Propounded By Anna Samuel
Off Calendar Jun-02-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ryan Ramseyer for Anna Samuel. Michael Martinovsky for Raisa Rikoshinsky.

Mr. Ramseyer stated that he is seeking clarification of the court's order as to the subpoena on the joint account, that they are seeking for account information from 2011 to date of death with the signature card to the account to see if no withdraw was made. Mr. Martinovsky stated that they have no objections to the separate account but he argued that his client has the right of privacy to the joint account. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the right of privacy and how the information is relevant. At the conclusion of the argument, the Court upheld the tentative ruling and instructed Mr. Martinovsky to prepare an order pursuant to the tentative ruling and Mr. Ramseyer to prepare an order narrowing down the scope of the documents that raises privacy concern. Both proposed orders are to be submitted by May 27, 2015. Mr. Ramseyer reported that mediation has not commenced due to his client lives in the east coast and she just had a baby and that she is unable to travel. He requested to extend mediation completion to September 21, 2015 and that he has requested a continuance of the next hearing date. Mr. Martionovsky argued that he is at a disadvantage for not able to respond to Mr. Rameseyer's proposed order. The Court stated that it may allow a letter brief from Mr. Martinovsky if he has any issues with Mr. Ramseyer's proposed order.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Yee. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-23-2015 Continued To May-27-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Stephen Picone for (petitioner) Ana Sammuel via court call.

Mr. Picone reported that his client had a baby, is unable to travel from New York, that he and Mr. Martinovsky signed a stipulation regarding continuing the hearing but does not know what happened to it, his client will be deposed next week, and they request extensions to May 11, 2015 for completion of mediation and May 22, 2015 to submit a status or joint statement. The court requested that Mr. Picone submit a proposed order regarding the extensions and the petition for letters of administration and the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary were continued to May 27, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Murillo. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-23-2015 Continued To May-27-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-03-2014 Continued To Feb-23-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Martinovsky for Raisa Rikoshinsky.

Mr. Martinovsky stated that Mr. Horsted is no longer in this matter, that other Counsel is in the case, that his Client's opinion is that the Estate should not have been administered, that there is $9,000.00 in the Estate which was spent on funeral expenses, that the Decedent and his Client got married and that two Doctors have stated that the Decedent was competent. The Court noted that opposing Counsel had requested an evidentiary hearing. An Order to Appear will be issued to Attorney Stephen Picone to appear on February 23, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court signed an order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than January 30, 2015. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by February 17, 2015. The Petition for Letters of Administration, and the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary were continued to and the Parties are to return on February 23, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Attorney Stephen Picone appeared after the matter had concluded. The Clerk advised Mr. Picone of the mediation order as well the completion date and the continued date. The Court will not issue the order to appear.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-30-2014 Continued To Jun-17-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Darrel Horsted for Anna Samuel. Elizabeth Veynberg for Raisa Rikoshinsky.

Discussion was held with regard to the value of the assets, the bank account that was held in joint tenancy, the two bank accounts that were held in the Decedent's name only, the lawsuit the Decedent had in 2006, appointment of a neutral Fiduciary with limited powers for the purpose of searching for/investigating assets and requesting tax returns, etc. The Petition for Letters of Administration was continued to June 17, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Ms. Veynberg to file a competing petition. Said petition is to be given the same hearing date of June 17, 2014.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)
